north-star.quality-gate: How does this manifest for ME?
north-star.feeling: premium · calm · editorial · private intelligence briefing
north-star.anti-feeling: cosmos backgrounds · esoteric kitsch · neon · cheap symbols · pop-astro generic

invariants.invariant: Today is the main MVP screen, route /day/:date. It is the screen of the SELECTED day, not always today.
Top flags and "Why for me" are inline UI on Today, not separate screens.
Calendar is navigation, not forecast. It does not show monthly readings.
7-day strip shows exactly 3 statuses: supportive | steady | tense.
Referral grants 14 days to both inviter and invitee. Bonus days are consumed BEFORE subscription.
There is NO permanent free tier. Access opens via subscription or invite.
Frontend does not interpret astrology, does not select top flags, does not compute day status, does not reassemble why-sections, does not receive raw SolarSage.
LLM receives only curated SemanticLayer, never raw SolarSage, never internal scoring fields.

LLM never calculates astrology. All chart factors, derived activations, scores, lots, fixed stars, special points, and house/sign placements come from SolarSage/collector outputs and backend scoring code only.

Scoring knowledge is canon data, not hidden code: spheres, dignity modifiers, aspect rules, activation rules, thresholds, and convergence bonuses must be versioned under grace/canon/*.yml before product scoring consumes them.

Agent orchestration is configured through grace/orchestrator/*, not hidden in Prefect flows or product backend code. Prefect is runtime only; GRACE semantics live in the project adapter, packet schema, role contracts, and verification profiles.

A coder agent must not start from a packet unless the packet has allowed write scope, frozen scope, verification requirements, expected evidence, and escalation triggers. Legacy packets may be warning-only until migrated, but new packets must follow packet.schema.json.
Every backend response carries meta with contract_version and applicable layer versions.
contract_version changes rarely. calculation/normalization/scoring/prompt versions can change without breaking the frontend.
Caches are separated by layer: natal_snapshots, period_snapshots, daily_snapshots, semantic_layers, today_payloads. Never mix raw and curated.
Every cache row carries input_hash that captures all inputs that, if changed, must invalidate the row.

use-case.title: Telegram WebApp authentication
use-case.trigger: App opens initData payload from Telegram WebApp.

main-flow.step: 4. Backend upserts users row by telegram_id.
5. Backend returns session token (HttpOnly cookie or JWT).

rules.rule: HMAC validation must reject expired or tampered initData.
Session lifetime: 30 days, sliding.

use-case.acceptance: Valid initData yields a session and a users row. Tampered initData yields 401 with no DB write.

use-case.title: Onboarding profile creation

main-flow.step: 1. User submits birth_date, birth_time, birth_place, current_location, optional birthday_location.
2. Backend validates and stores user_profiles row.
3. Backend invalidates any pre-existing snapshots for the user.

rules.rule: house_system defaults to "placidus" unless overridden.
Coordinates must include lat/lon/timezone for every location.
If birthday_location is omitted, current_location is used.

use-case.acceptance: After successful submit, GET /api/profile returns the saved data.

use-case.title: Edit birth or location data

rules.rule: Editing birth_date | birth_time | birth_place invalidates natal_snapshots, period_snapshots, daily_snapshots, semantic_layers, today_payloads for this user.
Editing current_location or birthday_location invalidates period_snapshots, daily_snapshots, semantic_layers, today_payloads.

use-case.acceptance: After edit, next /api/day/:date returns recomputed payload.

use-case.title: Compute access state for a date

main-flow.step: 3. Consume entries in that order against requested date.
4. Return state full | preview | locked plus reason and counters.

rules.rule: Bonus days are consumed BEFORE subscription days.
If user has no active entry: state = preview, reason = expired_access.

use-case.acceptance: For a user with 14 referral days starting today and no subscription: dates 0..13 → full, date 14 → preview.

use-case.title: View Today screen for a selected date

main-flow.step: 3. If preview: return preview TodayPayload (date, headline, partial flags, teaser, soft lock).
4. If full: lookup today_payloads by (user_id, date, contract_version). On hit return.
5. On miss: traverse pipeline (natal → period → daily → normalize → score → semantic → llm → payload), persist each layer, return payload.

rules.rule: Return must conform to C-TODAY-PAYLOAD (docs/05).
meta.cached must be true on cache hit, false on miss.

use-case.acceptance: Pilot acceptance (PHASE-1): for any onboarded user and any date in allowed range, response is a valid TodayPayload (fixture-backed) within 500ms p95.

main-flow.step: Tap arrow or swipe → push /day/:nextDate, fetch payload, scroll to top.

rules.rule: Date lives in URL.
User is identified by Telegram session, not by route.
Allowed range: previous, current, next month.
Disabled days are non-clickable.
Tap on available or locked day → /day/:date.
Calendar is navigation, not forecast (INV-CALENDAR-NAV).
Exactly 3 statuses (INV-WEEK-3STATUSES).
Internal qualities map to public statuses per docs/04 §11.

use-case.title: Invite a friend, both receive 14 days

rules.rule: On a new valid invitee signup, write 2 access_ledger rows (inviter + invitee), type=referral_bonus, days_amount=14.
Each user can be invited only once.
Locked day opens the same /day/:date.
Show date, headline, partial flags, teaser, soft lock.
Soft lock CTAs: subscribe, invite.

use-case.title: Render natal reading as versioned blocks

rules.rule: Frontend is a generic block renderer; unknown block.type is skipped, not crashed (docs/06 §1).
Routes: /readings/natal and /readings/natal/[section].
One checkin per (user_id, date), upsert on repeat.
note text is never sent to LLM unless prompt_version is note-aware.
yesterdayEcho appears in TodayPayload only when a checkin exists.
Successful payment writes access_ledger row type=subscription.
Subscription is consumed only after referral_bonus and gift days are exhausted.

business-rules.rule: Consumption order: referral_bonus → gift → subscription → adjustment.

Active access grants 1 subscription_weekly_free horary question per access week; it expires weekly and does not accumulate. Paid horary credits accumulate and do not expire unless explicitly configured.
Horary spend order: current subscription_weekly_free → expiring referral/gift/adjustment credits by nearest expires_at → paid credits oldest first.
UI shows exactly 3 day statuses; internal qualities map per docs/04 §11.
"Why for me" is inline disclosure, not a route.
Selected date must be in the URL.
Calendar allowed range = previous + current + next month.
Changing calculation/normalization/scoring/prompt does not change contract_version unless TodayPayload shape changes.
LLM output must never reference internal scoring fields (salience, supportScore, frictionScore, shadowRisk, internal ranking, raw JSON).
The existing deep SolarSage collector remains the reference implementation until parity tests prove the service/split implementation is equivalent on controller-approved golden charts.
Sphere salience for time-sensitive product output must include an activation/convergence layer; predictive raw factors cannot remain unused if they are collected and relevant to the target date.
New scoring code must not introduce hardcoded sphere tables, dignity constants, aspect thresholds, or top-N aspect cutoffs outside the versioned canon data files.
The orchestrator must be portable between projects: project-specific facts live under grace/orchestrator/* and generic runtime code must consume them through stable interfaces.

nfr.nfr: Snapshots retained 180 days unless invalidated by version bump.
checkin.note never logged in shared logs, never sent to LLM unless prompt is note-aware.
Every payload carries meta.generatedAt, meta.cached, all *_version fields.
For the same birth input, target date, SolarSage version, and scoring canon version, derived scores and activation evidence are byte-stable or fixture-approved.
All UI is mobile-first; long-text reading mode tuned for 360-440px.
Touch targets ≥ 44px; contrast ≥ WCAG AA on body text.

open-questions.question: Is the 5th tab "Ask" in scope for MVP?
Confirm trial = 14 days for both inviter and invitee.
Telegram Stars vs external provider for PHASE-6.
